jobs.name: Daily Work Log
jobs.prompt: Create a work log for yesterday (the day before today). Follow the work-log skill exactly. "Today" for the purposes of this run means yesterday's date — pre-compute it as `date -d "yesterday" +%F`. All source queries, date filters, and the daily note lookup should use yesterday's date. Overwrite any existing Work Log block in the note (do not prompt for confirmation — this is an automated run).

jobs.name: Morning Briefing — 5AM daily
jobs.prompt:
[IMPORTANT: You are running as a scheduled cron job. DELIVERY: Your final response will be automatically delivered to the user — do NOT use send_message or try to deliver the output yourself. Just produce your report/output as your final response and the system handles the rest.]

Morning briefing prep processes are complete. Please generate today's morning briefing report as a single message following the simplified format described in the morning-briefing skill:

• Calendar preview (just today and tomorrow)
• Summary of yesterday's work log (not the full log)
• High-urgency tasks and inbox candidates
• Calendar candidates

At the end of the report, ask me if I have any follow-up questions or requests.

jobs.name: Unified Brain Feeds Ingest
jobs.prompt:
[IMPORTANT: You are running as a scheduled cron job. DELIVERY: Your final response will be automatically delivered to the user — do NOT use send_message or try to deliver the output yourself. Just produce your report/output as your final response and the system handles the rest. SILENT: If there is genuinely nothing new to report, respond with exactly "[SILENT]" (nothing else) to suppress delivery. Never combine [SILENT] with content — either report your findings normally, or say [SILENT] and nothing more.]

## Script Output
The following data was collected by a pre-run script. Use it as context for your analysis.

The output of the fetcher script `fetch_unified_ingest.py` is injected below:

---
If all of `slack`, `linear`, `emails`, and `telegram` outputs are empty or have no items, stop immediately. Output absolutely nothing (or just '[SILENT]') and terminate. Do not write any files, do not edit daily notes, and do not send any message.

---
### 1. Process Slack Captures (`slack`)
If there are items under the `slack` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the reaction type: `jg_log` or `jg_decision` based on the `"reaction_type"` field in the JSON item.
2. Determine the date of the conversation (from the `ts` or messages timestamps) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
3. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
4. **Determine Target Folder and Path based on Reaction Type:**
   - **Path A: If reaction_type is "jg_log" (standard Slack log):**
     - Since this is an **Input**, bypass the inbox! Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Slack/`
     - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Slack - [Title].md`
     - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
       ```yaml
       ---
       id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts
       daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
       category: "[[Slack]]"
       channel: "<channel_name>"
       original_url: "<permalink>"
       participants:
         - "<Participant A>"
         - "<Participant B>"
       ---
       ```
     - **Body Layout:**
       Keep standard Slack logs minimalist. No point-by-point summaries, thoughts, or decisions in the body. Only:
       ```markdown
       # 📥 Slack Capture: [Title]

       - **Channel:** #<channel_name>
       - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
       - **Original Link:** [Slack Thread](<permalink>)

       ## Topic Description
       <A brief, 1-2 sentence Topic Description placeholder to be expanded later during Vault Jam.>
       ```
   - **Path B: If reaction_type is "jg_decision" (Decision note):**
     - Since this is a maturity-ladder note (not an Input), save it under `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inbox/` for review first!
     - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Decision - [Title].md`
     - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
       ```yaml
       ---
       id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on message ts
       daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
       category: "[[Decisions]]"
       ---
       ```
     - **Body Layout:**
       ```markdown
       # 🏛 Decision: [Title]

       - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
       - **Original Link:** [Slack Thread](<permalink>)

       ## Context
       <Summarize the context of the decision from the Slack thread.>

       ## Options & Trade-offs
       <Extract pros/cons, options, and tradeoffs discussed in the thread.>

       ## Resolution
       <Detail the finalized decision and resolution, attributing it clearly to the actual decision-maker.>
       ```
5. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Slack message as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_slack_brains.py --mark-processed <channel_id> <ts>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py <ingest_rel_path> [--gist "..."]` (e.g., `Inputs/Slack/<filename>` or `Inbox/<filename>`) to sync project hub states.

---
### 2. Process Linear Captures (`linear`)
If there are items under the `linear` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the date of the comment/update (from its `createdAt` timestamp) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
2. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
3. Since this is an **Input**, bypass the inbox! Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Linear/`
4. Generate the Note Content:
   - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Linear - [Title].md`
   - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
     ```yaml
     ---
     id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on createdAt
     daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
     category: "[[Linear]]"
     original_url: "<permalink>"
     author: "<author_name>"
     associated_with: "<external_link_to_linear_issue_or_doc>"
     ---
     ```
   - **Note Body Layout:**
     ```markdown
     # 📥 Linear Capture: [Title]

     - **Author:** <author_name>
     - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD
     - **Original Link:** [Linear Link](<permalink>)
     - **Associated With:** [<issue_id_or_title>](<external_link_to_linear_issue_or_doc>)

     ## Topic Description
     <A high-quality 2-3 sentence Topic Description synthesizing the comment/update, the parent issue/document/project/initiative context, and its corresponding thread.>
     ```
     *Note on link formatting:* The `Associated With` section and frontmatter field MUST use an **external link** (e.g. Markdown external link `[PROD-468 - Animate correct answers in lessons](https://linear.app/...)`) pointing directly to the relevant issue, project, initiative, or doc in Linear. Do NOT use Obsidian wikilinks like `[[PROD-468]]`.
5. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Linear item as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_linear_brains.py --mark-processed <id>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py Inputs/Linear/<filename> [--gist "..."]` to sync project hub states.

---
### 3. Process Email Dispatches (`emails`)
If there are items under the `emails` key, process them one by one:
For each email, load the `apollo-brain-ingest` skill to handle dual-action dispatch (File it, Task it, or both):
1. Load message context using `python3 ~/.hermes/skills/note-taking/apollo-brain-ingest/scripts/load_context.py <id>`.
2. Follow the `apollo-brain-ingest` skill to parse instructions and determine intents:
   - If filing, save directly to `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Emails/YYYY-MM-DD - Spaced Subject.md` (or `Inbox/` only if explicitly specified by Justin's instruction like "File in inbox"). Since this is an Input, we bypass the inbox by default.
   - After filing, run light integration (append to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py <ingest_rel_path> [--gist "..."]` to sync hub pages.
3. If tasking:
   - Note: Todoist is disconnected. Create a TaskNote under `TaskNotes/Tasks/` following the `tasknotes` schema (status: `open`, `now`, etc.).

---
### 4. Process Telegram Captures (`telegram`)
If there are items under the `telegram` key, process them one by one:
1. Determine the date of the conversation (from the `started_at` ISO string) in YYYY-MM-DD format.
2. Determine a spaced capitalized Title based on the topic.
3. Since this is an **Input**, bypass the inbox! Save the file under: `/home/justin.guest/vault/Inputs/Telegram/`
4. Generate the Note Content:
   - **Filename:** `YYYY-MM-DD - Telegram - [Title].md`
   - **Properties (Frontmatter):**
     ```yaml
     ---
     id: '<timestamp_id>'  # YYYYMMDDHHmmss format based on started_at
     daily_note: "[[Daily Notes/YYYY-MM-DD Weekday|YYYY-MM-DD Weekday]]"
     category: "[[Telegram]]"
     ---
     ```
   - **Note Body Layout:**
     ```markdown
     # 📥 Telegram Capture: [Title]

     - **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD

     ## Summary
     <A concise, high-quality markdown summary of the Telegram chat session synthesizing the main discussion, key takeaways, and conclusions.>
     ```
     *Crucial rule:* Do NOT include any transcript highlights or transcript sections. Only the `Summary` section is needed.
5. **Integrations:**
   - Run the command to mark the Telegram session as processed:
     `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/fetch_telegram_brains.py --mark-processed <session_id>`
   - Run the light integration pass (appending to `Utilities/log.md` with daily note link).
   - Run `python3 ~/.hermes/scripts/integrate_entities.py Inputs/Telegram/<filename> [--gist "..."]` to sync project hub states.

---
### 5. Reporting
At the end, report on all items processed from Slack, Linear, Emails, and Telegram. Keep descriptions concise and formatted for Telegram delivery.

jobs.name: compile-inputs
jobs.last_error:
RuntimeError: # Compile Inputs Cron Job Execution Report

## Execution Summary

- **Mode:** `since-last-run`
- **Cutoff (`last_run_at` before):** `2026-07-21T10:00:31-04:00` [High]
- **Current System Time:** `2026-07-21T11:30:31-04:00`
- **Phase 0 Status:** Applied 5 ready proposals successfully. [High]
- **Phase 1 (Readings) Status:** No-op. No unprocessed readings found. [High]
- **Phase 2 (Sources) Status:** No-op. No new sources created after cutoff. [High]
- **Phase 3 (Meetings) Status:** No-op. No new non-chunk meetings created after cutoff. [High]
- **Phase 4 (Others Digest) Status:** Interrupted. Found 7 eligible inputs (Scraps and Slack thread captures) created after the cutoff; however, execution was halted due to agent tool-calling limits before writing the digest proposal. [High]

---

## Phase 0: Applied Proposals Detail

The `apply_proposals.py` operations script was executed to batch-apply all files in `Inbox/Ready to Apply/`. 

### Summary of Actions
- **Applied Proposals:** 5
- **New Note Creates:** 7 notes created in `Inbox/Notes/` (awaiting triage).
- **In-Place Enrichments:** 5 existing notes updated surgically in `Notebook/` and `Inbox/Notes/`.
- **Archived Proposals:** All 5 applied proposals moved to `Utilities/Review/`.
- **Knowledge Log:** Append-only logs written successfully to `Utilities/ops/knowledge-log.md`.

### Applied Items Detail

1. **`Proposal - Emma, Anya, Bruna, Justin 20260721103100`**
   - **Creates:** `Inbox/Notes/Qualitative testing: Framework for reading reactions 20260721103101.md`
   - **Enriched in place:** None

2. **`Proposal - Extreme Is the Easy Way Out 20260721103000`**
   - **Creates:** `Inbox/Notes/Extremism means avoiding active judgment 20260721103001.md`
   - **Enriched in place:** `Notebook/Self help self loathing and self control 20260128134547.md`

3. **`Proposal - How Figma Built a Product OS That Saved PMs 20260720131514`**
   - **Creates:** `Inbox/Notes/Figma's PM OS 20260720131517.md`, `Inbox/Notes/PM Operating Systems 20260720131518.md`
   - **Enriched in place:** `Notebook/Projects/Justin OS 20260718101747.md`

4. **`Proposal - How I AI GPT-5.6 Local AI and Agent Harness 20260720131510`**
   - **Creates:** `Inbox/Notes/Agent harness 20260720131510.md`
   - **Enriched in place:** `Notebook/Prefer CLI to MCP 20260713182600.md`, `Notebook/Agent-manager vs agent-prompter 20260708122800.md` *(Note: Skipped body merge on the latter as there was no fenced markdown in the enrich guidance).*

5. **`Proposal - How to Build an Automated Software Factory With AI Agents 20260720131509`**
   - **Creates:** `Inbox/Notes/AI software factories 20260720131509.md`, `Inbox/Notes/ChatPRD's AI software factory 20260720131511.md`
   - **Enriched in place:** `Inbox/Notes/AI software factories 20260720131509.md`

---

## Phase 4: Pending Triage (Interrupted)

The tool-calling limit was reached before a digest proposal could be drafted and saved to `Inbox/Proposals/`. The following 7 eligible inputs are unprocessed and stand ready to be compiled into a single theme-clustered digest proposal on the next run [High]:

### Clustered Themes & Inputs Identified:

#### Theme A: Flashcard Prototype Design & Product Strategy (Slack Capture)
- **`Inputs/Slack/2026-07-21 - Slack - Flashcard Prototype Gamification Spectrum Analogy.md`**
  - *Context:* Tor frames the balance of learning efficiency and engagement as a "health food vs. candy" spectrum.
- **`Inputs/Slack/2026-07-21 - Slack - Flashcard Prototype Health Food Vs Candy Analogy.md`**
  - *Context:* Justin refutes the candy analogy, arguing games are defined by artificial rules and contests, whereas candy offers ruleless value.
- **`Inputs/Slack/2026-07-21 - Slack - Flashcard Prototype Tool Game Tradeoffs.md`**
  - *Context:* Trade-offs on the tool-to-game spectrum; maintaining creativity while respecting these constraints.
- **`Inputs/Slack/2026-07-21 - Slack - Flashcard Prototype Evolving Beyond First Ideas.md`**
  - *Context:* Multiple core loop modes and evolving beyond initial concepts using the prototype.

#### Theme B: User Testing & Outreach
- **`Inputs/Scraps/Friends-and-family testing for flashcard prototype 1 20260721101447.md`**
  - *Context:* Log of outreach to book club members, family, and peers for early feedback.

#### Theme C: Vault Schema & Systems Alignment
- **`Inputs/Scraps/Removed note-level status from vault schema 20260721105557.md`**
  - *Context:* Post-mortem of removing lifecycle `status` (draft/final) in favor of directory-based lifecycle trust, updating docs/rules/hygiene.
- **`Inputs/Scraps/Contact name fidelity for Kristina and Endre 20260721105606.md`**
  - *Context:* Establishing traps for SignLab contact name misspellings (e.g., Christina → Kristina Kennedy, Andre → Endre Elvestad) within instructions, rules, and compile workflows.

---

## Next Steps

1. **Triage Drafts:** Triage the 7 newly created drafts waiting inside `Inbox/Notes/`.
2. **Compile Digest:** On the next cron tick or manual compile-inputs run, Phase 4 will pickup and write a theme-clustered digest proposal covering the 7 listed inputs and set their `notebook_proposal` fields accordingly.
